fix_plans.title: Stratified batch sampling for minority ethnicity groups
fix_plans.content: In train.py, replace the unshuffled-permutation DataLoader for train_ds with a WeightedRandomSampler that oversamples low-prevalence ethnicity groups so every batch has comparable ethnic coverage, which lifts TPR for the laggard groups (black/hispanic) without touching the probe or the threshold. Concretely: import torch.utils.data.WeightedRandomSampler and torch.Generator; build per-sample weights from train_ds.eth via w_i = 1.0 / N_g(i) where g(i) is argmax of eth row i and N_g is the train-set count of group g (so asian/hispanic/black, which are tiny, contribute ~10x the weight of white). Sampler replacement=False, num_samples=len(train_ds) (so epoch length unchanged), generator=torch.Generator().manual_seed(SEED) for determinism. Drop shuffle=True from DataLoader and pass the sampler instead. val_loader stays shuffle=False (probe requires it). The expected effect: the model sees hispanic/black positives much more often per epoch, so its decision boundary at THRESHOLD=0.5 shifts toward predicting more positives for them, raising their TPR from ~0.54/0.59 toward the white/asian band and shrinking max-min gap. val AUPRC anchor should be preserved because oversampling underrepresented groups also increases the gradient signal on the rare positive class; monitor that anchor so AUPRC does not collapse.

fix_plans.title: Add equal-opportunity fairness penalty to BCE loss
fix_plans.content: In train.py, modify train_one_epoch to add an equal-opportunity penalty term that penalizes per-batch TPR variance across ethnicity groups, directly targeting the probe metric at the training objective. Concretely: inside train_one_epoch, after logits = model(features), compute per-row y_true and per-row group_idx = batch['features'][:, -5:].argmax(dim=1) (the eth block is concatenated at the end of features by dataset.py — verify the last 5 columns are ETH_NAMES in the same order as prober.py). Compute a soft TPR_g = (sigmoid(logits) * labels * group_onehot).sum(dim=0) / ((labels * group_onehot).sum(dim=0).clamp_min(1.0)), giving a 5-vector of per-group batch TPRs (only groups present in the batch contribute meaningfully; protect with a presence mask where (labels * group_onehot).sum(dim=0) >= 1). fairness_loss = TPR_g[present].var() (unbiased=False), and add 0.05 * fairness_loss to the BCE loss before backward(). This penalizes variance in per-group TPR each batch, pulling the model toward equal-opportunity behavior without touching the BCEWithLogitsLoss(pos_weight=...) signal. The expected effect: because the penalty matches the probe definition, training moves TPR_g toward parity, collapsing the max-min gap from the 0.29 band toward the 0.05 standard. The AUPRC anchor is preserved because the BCE term still dominates and the penalty weight 0.05 is small; if it overfires, monitor the AUPRC anchor. This is a regularization angle, distinct from sampling (plan 1) and architecture (plan 3).

fix_plans.title: Add learnable per-ethnicity bias term to the model
fix_plans.content: In train.py, modify the LogisticRegression model to include a learnable per-ethnicity bias offset added to the shared logit, so the model can equalize per-group TPR at the frozen THRESHOLD=0.5 by learning group-specific offsets. Concretely, in LogisticRegression.__init__ add self.group_bias = nn.Parameter(torch.zeros(5)); in forward, after self.linear(x).squeeze(-1) returns shape (B,), compute group_idx = x[:, -5:].argmax(dim=1) (the eth block is concatenated at the end of features by dataset.py) and return logits + self.group_bias[group_idx]. This adds 5 free parameters that the optimizer can use to shift each ethnicity's operating point at the frozen threshold. Initialize to zero so round-1 behavior is identical at init and the bias only moves as fairness gradients flow. Optimizer already covers model.parameters() so no optim change is needed. The expected effect: because the probe metric is literally max_g TPR_g - min_g TPR_g at the frozen THRESHOLD=0.5, per-group additive logit offsets can directly move each group's effective threshold, so the optimizer will push group_bias for low-TPR groups (hispanic/black) upward and high-TPR groups (asian/other) slightly downward, collapsing the gap. The shared linear layer still learns the predictive signal so val AUPRC anchor is preserved; group_bias is a thin correction layer, not a replacement for the main model. This is an architecture angle, distinct from sampling (plan 1) and loss regularization (plan 2).
